perturbation.original_text: Any Confidential Information disclosed to a third party pursuant to this Section shall be provided pursuant to a non-disclosure agreement between the party providing the information and the third party, which non-disclosure agreement shall substantially conform to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Any Confidential Information disclosed to a third party pursuant to this Section shall be provided pursuant to a non-disclosure agreement between the party providing the information and the third party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'which non-disclosure agreement shall substantially conform to this Agreement', the requirement for the third-party non-disclosure agreement to align with the standards of the original agreement is eliminated. This creates uncertainty. Other sections assume a standard of confidentiality equivalent to this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party undertakes to cause any of its employees to whom such Confidential Information is transmitted to be bound to the same obligation of secrecy and confidentiality to which the parties are bound under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the third-party NDA to 'substantially conform to this Agreement.' By removing this requirement in the changed text, the third party's confidentiality obligations are no longer explicitly tied to the standards of the original agreement. The contradicted text states that employees to whom Confidential Information is transmitted must be bound to 'the same obligation of secrecy and confidentiality to which the parties are bound under this Agreement.' If the third-party NDA doesn't have to conform to the original agreement, there is no guarantee that the third party's confidentiality obligations are equivalent, creating a contradiction because the standard is no longer consistent.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of this Agreement shall terminate with respect to any particular portion of a party's Confidential Information: (i) if either party can show that the Confidential Information received from the other is or has become generally available to the public through no violation of the terms of this Agreement; *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> (ii) if either party can show that such Confidential Information is in a written record in such party's files prior to receipt from the other party; (iii) if either party at any time lawfully obtains such Confidential Information in writing from a third party under circumstances permitting its disclosure; (iv) if such Confidential Information is disclosed with the prior written consent of the party to whom such Confidential Information belongs, provided that any disclosure complies in all respects with the terms of such written consent; or (v) if such Confidential Information is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a governmental agency or required by operation of law; provided that the party to whom such Confidential Information belongs shall be given written notice prior to such disclosure and such disclosure shall be permitted only to the extent required by law.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of this Agreement shall terminate with respect to any particular portion of a party's Confidential Information: (i) if either party can show that the Confidential Information received from the other is or has become generally available to the public; *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> (ii) if either party can show that such Confidential Information is in a written record in such party's files prior to receipt from the other party; (iii) if either party at any time lawfully obtains such Confidential Information in writing from a third party under circumstances permitting its disclosure; (iv) if such Confidential Information is disclosed with the prior written consent of the party to whom such Confidential Information belongs, provided that any disclosure complies in all respects with the terms of such written consent; or (v) if such Confidential Information is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a governmental agency or required by operation of law; provided that the party to whom such Confidential Information belongs shall be given written notice prior to such disclosure and such disclosure shall be permitted only to the extent required by law.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'through no violation of the terms of this Agreement', the confidentiality obligations terminate even if the information becomes public due to a breach of the agreement. Section 1 states that the parties should maintain the information in confidence and not make use of it other than for the benefit of the other party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that any disclosure of the other's Confidential Information other than for the benefit of the other party will be wrongful and will cause irreparable injury to the other party and, therefore, each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential information in strictest confidence and not to make use of it other than for the benefit of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the confidentiality obligations terminate if the information becomes public through no violation of the agreement. The changed text removes this condition, so the confidentiality obligations terminate even if the information becomes public due to a breach of the agreement. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential information in strictest confidence and not to make use of it other than for the benefit of the other party.

perturbation.original_text: Otherwise, the obligations of this Agreement with respect to either party's Confidential Information shall terminate on the later of (i) three (3) years after the Expiration Date (or earlier termination date) of this Agreement as set forth in Section 5 hereof, or (ii) in the event there are any contracts or agreements between the parties which are entered into in connection with information disclosed under this Agreement, three (3) years after the date of termination or expiration of all such contracts and agreements between the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Otherwise, the obligations of this Agreement with respect to either party's Confidential Information shall terminate three (3) years after the Expiration Date (or earlier termination date) of this Agreement as set forth in Section 5 hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(ii) in the event there are any contracts or agreements between the parties which are entered into in connection with information disclosed under this Agreement, three (3) years after the date of termination or expiration of all such contracts and agreements between the parties.', confidentiality will now terminate 3 years after the expiration date, even if contracts entered based on the agreement are still active, this creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that all Confidential Information which has or will come into its possession or knowledge after the Effective Date in connection with business discussions, conferences or other activities in pursuit of a business relationship between Federal and IBS: (i) is proprietary to the disclosing party, having been designed, developed or accumulated by the disclosing party at a great expense and over lengthy periods of time and *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> (ii) is secret, confidential and unique, and constitutes the exclusive property of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that confidentiality obligations terminate based on whichever is later: 3 years after the expiration date, or 3 years after the termination of agreements entered based on information disclosed under the agreement. The changed text removes the latter condition. The contradicted text states that the confidential information remains the exclusive property of the disclosing party. The removal of the second condition in the changed text creates a contradiction, as it is possible that information disclosed under this agreement can be used to enter into other contracts or agreements with the parties, and the confidential information can be disclosed even after 3 years, thereby contradicting the initial statement and creating uncertainty, and thus, a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall govern all communications between Federal and IBS that are made from the Effective Date of this Agreement through and including the date which shall be three (3) years from the Effective Date of this Agreement (the "Expiration Date").
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall govern all communications between Federal and IBS that are made from the Effective Date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'through and including the date which shall be three (3) years from the Effective Date of this Agreement (the "Expiration Date")' causes a contradiction because the agreement now seems to govern all communications indefinitely, conflicting with Section 4 that specifies termination conditions after three years. The Expiration Date is no longer clearly defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Otherwise, the obligations of this Agreement with respect to either party's Confidential Information shall terminate three (3) years after the Expiration Date (or earlier termination date) of this Agreement as set forth in Section 5 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified an 'Expiration Date' three years from the Effective Date. The changed text removes the Expiration Date definition, making the agreement seemingly perpetual. This contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that obligations regarding Confidential Information terminate three years after the 'Expiration Date' as defined in Section 5, creating an inconsistency in the contract. Section 5 now references an Expiration Date that is not defined in the updated Section.

perturbation.original_text: In addition to and not in lieu of the right to terminate as provided in Section 5, the parties agree that in the event of any violation or threatened *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> violation of this Agreement the injured party shall be authorized and entitled to obtain from any court of competent jurisdiction preliminary and permanent injunctive relief as well as an equitable accounting of all profits or benefits arising from such violation, which rights and remedies shall be cumulative and in addition to any other rights or remedies at law or in equity to which the injured party may be entitled.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that in the event of any violation or threatened *** Portions of this exhibit have been redacted pursuant to a Confidential Treatment Request. <PAGE> violation of this Agreement the injured party shall be authorized and entitled to obtain from any court of competent jurisdiction preliminary and permanent injunctive relief as well as an equitable accounting of all profits or benefits arising from such violation, which rights and remedies shall be cumulative and in addition to any other rights or remedies at law or in equity to which the injured party may be entitled.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'In addition to and not in lieu of the right to terminate as provided in Section 5', the availability of injunctive relief becomes unclear as it's no longer explicitly stated to be independent of the termination right in Section 5. With the Expiration Date removed from Section 5, Section 7 might be interpreted as the sole remedy for breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree that in the event of any breach of this Agreement by a party, the injured party shall have the right to immediately terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the right to injunctive relief was 'in addition to and not in lieu of' the termination right in Section 5. By removing this phrase, the changed text now implies that injunctive relief and the termination right in Section 5 might be mutually exclusive or that Section 5 is the exclusive remedy. The contradicted text states that a party has the right to terminate upon breach. The changed text, by omitting the original clarifying language, creates ambiguity as to whether injunctive relief is available in addition to termination, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction and affecting the remedies available to the injured party. Therefore the change creates a strong and clear contradiction.
